title: Candidate pairwise query-conditioned token stats rerun

queries: token similarity statistics candidate scorer scalable multiclass ranking
query conditioned token interaction non autoregressive candidate ranking

evidence.why_now: EXP144 was implementation-blocked / abnormal rather than scientifically resolved because the new token-stats path failed to initialize its query/key projections on item-incoterms and the first serial task was externally terminated before any evaluation. The token-stats hypothesis remains justified by EXP099, EXP138, and EXP141, so the correct next action is a clean rerun that consumes only the projection-init fix.

hypothesis: If the missing scalable signal is carried by token-level similarity peaks and spread rather than by a single pooled label vector, then augmenting the fast pairwise scorer with query-conditioned token-statistics residuals should recover part of the salt-side candidate-token benefit while staying in a non-autoregressive O(L + C*T) regime that remains plausible for Stage 4 pretraining.
notes: Keep Stage 3 finetune-only; do not introduce pretrain, loss-family changes, sampling changes, or packaging changes.
This is a clean rerun of EXP144 after fixing the missing query/key projection initialization in the token-stats scorer.
No scientific change relative to EXP144; the rerun exists only to recover the intended evidence after the implementation fix.
Launched on 2026-05-09 through the local Stage 3 launcher.
Completed on 2026-05-09 as an abnormal-run rather than a clean scientific bundle.
EXP148 and EXP149 both produced usable metrics, but EXP147 was again externally terminated by SIGTERM before any evaluation while using the 4-GPU serial placement that included GPU 0.
Do not use EXP147 as scientific evidence against the token-stats family; rerun cleanly with an explicit safer serial GPU placement that avoids the unstable GPU 0 pattern.
